title: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
description: Question batch for the Design Cost-Optimized Architectures domain.

questions.question: Which EC2 pricing option is often best for steady-state workloads?
questions.explanation: Steady workloads often benefit from commitment-based discounts.

questions.question: Which EC2 option can offer the lowest price for interruptible workloads?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances provide deep discounts for interruptible workloads.

questions.question: Which S3 feature can move old data to cheaper storage classes automatically?
questions.explanation: Lifecycle policies transition data to lower-cost storage classes over time.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps set spending thresholds and alerts?
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets lets teams monitor cost or usage against thresholds.

questions.question: Which AWS tool helps analyze historical cloud spending?
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer visualizes historical cost and usage trends.

questions.question: Which service choice often reduces operational overhead for relational databases?
questions.explanation: RDS reduces administrative work like patching and backups.

questions.question: Why is rightsizing important in AWS architectures?
answers: It reduces waste by matching resources to demand
It increases support plan level automatically
questions.explanation: Rightsizing improves cost efficiency by reducing overprovisioning.

questions.question: Which practice best helps allocate costs by project or team?
questions.explanation: Tagging resources improves cost visibility and chargeback.

questions.question: Which architecture pattern helps avoid paying for idle capacity?
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling matches capacity more closely to current demand.

questions.question: Which compute model can reduce costs for intermittent event-driven workloads?
questions.explanation: Serverless compute avoids paying for idle server capacity.

questions.question: Which storage choice is most cost-effective for rarely accessed long-term archives?
questions.explanation: Archive storage classes reduce cost for infrequently retrieved data.

questions.question: Why should unused resources be identified and removed regularly?
questions.explanation: Idle or forgotten resources still contribute to spend without business value.

questions.question: Which design can reduce data transfer and compute cost for repeated reads?
answers: Sending every request to the database
questions.explanation: Caching can reduce repeated backend processing and transfer.

questions.question: What is a key reason to use managed services in cost-optimized architectures?
answers: They always eliminate all security work
questions.explanation: Managed services can reduce the effort and staffing overhead needed to operate infrastructure.

questions.question: Which AWS report provides detailed usage and billing line items?
questions.explanation: The Cost and Usage Report contains detailed billing and usage data.

questions.question: Which purchase model should be avoided for critical workloads that cannot be interrupted unexpectedly?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances can be interrupted and are not ideal for inflexible critical workloads.

questions.question: Which architecture choice can lower cost for development environments used only during office hours?
questions.explanation: Stopping nonproduction resources when unused reduces waste.

questions.question: Why are multiple AWS accounts useful for cost optimization?
answers: They improve financial visibility and ownership
They reduce every service price automatically
They remove the need for tags
questions.explanation: Separate accounts can improve visibility, budget control, and ownership boundaries.

questions.question: Which design best balances cost and resilience for steady production web traffic?
answers: Commit base capacity and scale the rest elastically
Run only On-Demand at fixed high capacity
Use only Spot Instances for everything
questions.explanation: A mixed approach can reduce cost while preserving resilience and flexibility.

questions.question: Why is architecture review important for long-term cost optimization?
answers: Workloads and usage patterns change over time
questions.explanation: Cost optimization is continuous because business and technical needs evolve.

questions.question: Which approach best reduces compute cost for fault-tolerant batch jobs?
questions.explanation: Fault-tolerant batch workloads are strong candidates for discounted interruptible compute.

questions.question: Which choice best supports cost-efficient storage for data with changing access patterns?
answers: Use lifecycle policies and appropriate storage classes
Keep all data on the most expensive tier
questions.explanation: Lifecycle transitions align storage cost with real access needs.

questions.question: What is the main cost risk of overprovisioning?
questions.explanation: Overprovisioned resources increase spend without equivalent value.

questions.question: Which architecture choice can reduce egress costs for frequently accessed static content globally?
answers: Use a CDN and cache content effectively
Serve all content from one private server
questions.explanation: Effective caching can reduce repeated origin transfers and improve delivery efficiency.

questions.question: Why is deleting unattached storage important?
answers: Unused storage can still incur charges
questions.explanation: Unattached storage resources may continue generating cost if left in place.

questions.question: Which option best supports economical nonproduction experimentation?
answers: Use smaller, temporary resources and remove them when done
Use the largest instances by default
questions.explanation: Temporary, appropriately sized resources keep nonproduction cost under control.

questions.question: Which practice most directly improves accountability for cloud spend?
answers: Assign ownership and use cost allocation tags
questions.explanation: Ownership and tagging make spend easier to track and manage.

questions.question: What is the best reason to review idle load balancers, IPs, and storage periodically?
answers: They may create unnecessary ongoing cost
questions.explanation: Orphaned resources can quietly add cost over time.

questions.question: Which design best supports cost-efficient high availability for many web workloads?
answers: Use managed services, elasticity, and right-sized resources
Use one shared environment for all data
questions.explanation: Managed services plus elasticity often improve both availability and cost efficiency.

questions.question: Which action most directly helps prevent surprise monthly cloud bills?
answers: Budget alerts and regular cost review
questions.explanation: Early alerts and regular review provide time to correct unexpected spending.
